toast.needPosted: Posted. Your need is live on the board.
toast.offerPosted: Posted. Your offer is live on the board.
toast.projectCreated: Project created. Add tasks and launch it when you're ready.
toast.exchangeConfirmedPending: Confirmed. Waiting for the other person to confirm too.
toast.exchangeConfirmedComplete: Confirmed. Credits have flowed between you.

common.copied: Copied. Share it over Signal, in person, or on paper.
common.copyFailed: Couldn't access the clipboard — select the link above and copy manually.

drafts.bannerMessage: You started one of these {{when}}. Continue where you left off, or start fresh?

profileNudge.label: Profile completion
profileNudge.message: Members can find you for in-person help if you add a neighborhood or a skill or two. Optional, but it helps.

firstActionNudge.label: Getting started
firstActionNudge.message: Two ways in: tap any post that catches your eye to see how exchange works, or share something you need or can offer. No pressure to post first — lurking is welcome too.

projects.empty: No active projects yet. A project is a collective goal — multiple tasks, multiple contributors. The first one is always the hardest to start.

sparkline.label: Project contributions over the last {{days}} days; {{hours}} hours total.
sparkline.caption: Last {{days}} days · {{hours}}h contributed

create.title: Start a project
create.subtitle: A shared goal that needs more than one person and more than one task.
create.fieldTargetHoursHint: Total hours you think this will take, all tasks combined.
create.errorTitle: Give the project a short title.
create.errorHours: Target hours must be a positive number.
create.errorDeadlineDays: Days must be a whole number greater than 0, or blank for no deadline.

detail.noTasks: No tasks yet. The organizer can add the first one below.
detail.launchHint: Move from draft to active. Members can claim tasks once you launch.

task.needsMoreHandsTooltip: This task could use additional support. Anyone can step in to help.

postForm.subtitleNeed: What would support look like right now?
postForm.subtitleOffer: What can you share with the community?
postForm.fieldHoursHint: One hour of help = one hour of credit, whatever the work.
postForm.placeholderTitleOffer: e.g. Extra soup and bread this week
postForm.placeholderDescription: Add any helpful details — timing, location zone, accessibility, etc.
postForm.errorNeedTitle: Give your post a short title so people know what it's about.
postForm.errorHoursPositive: Estimated hours must be a positive number.
postForm.errorExpiresInDays: Days must be a whole number greater than 0, or blank to never expire.

postDetail.notFound: This post couldn't be found. It may have been cancelled.
postDetail.actionsWaiting: Waiting for someone to claim this.
postDetail.actionsExplain: When the help has actually happened, both of you confirm below. Credits transfer once both have confirmed.
postDetail.actionsConfirmed: You've confirmed. Waiting on the other party.
postDetail.actionsClaimedBy: This post has been claimed. {{helper}} is helping {{helped}}.
postDetail.actionsCompleted: Completed. {{hours}} of credit flowed from {{helped}} to {{helper}}. Thank you both.
postDetail.actionsDisputed: This exchange has been flagged for community review. A mediator will follow up.
postDetail.dialogClaimNeedDescription: You're offering to help with {{hours}} of {{category}}. You'll earn credit once both of you confirm completion.
postDetail.dialogClaimOfferDescription: You'd like to receive help for {{hours}}. Credits transfer when both parties confirm.
postDetail.dialogCompleteDescription: Confirm that the help actually happened. Credits transfer once both parties confirm. If something went wrong, you can flag it instead.
postDetail.dialogDisputeTitle: Flag this exchange for community review?
postDetail.dialogDisputeDescription: The exchange will be flagged and a community mediator can help resolve it. Your credits won't transfer yet.
postDetail.dialogCancelDescription: Cancelled posts stay visible but can't be claimed.
postDetail.dialogReleaseDescription: This will reopen the post so someone else can step in.

lockScreen.title: This device is locked
lockScreen.introWith: Enter your passphrase to unlock {{name}}'s identity on this device.
lockScreen.introNone: Enter your passphrase to unlock this device.
lockScreen.wrongPassphrase: That passphrase didn't match. Try again.
lockScreen.nothingToUnlock: No wrapped keys on this device — nothing to unlock. (If you're seeing this, reload the page.)
lockScreen.noRecoveryNote: Forgotten passphrases cannot be recovered — by design. If you've lost yours, the only path is Profile → Emergency → Hard purge and starting over with a fresh identity.

invite.wantsYou: {{name}} wants you in their mutual aid network.
invite.fingerprintReminder: Before accepting: confirm that the inviter-key fingerprint above matches what {{name}} shared with you in person or over a secure channel. A link that reached you via untrusted email is worth a double-check.
invite.welcome: Welcome in. Redirecting to the board…
invite.displayNameRequired: Choose a display name or pseudonym to continue.

errors.malformed: This invite link is missing or damaged. Ask the inviter to send a fresh one.
errors.expired: This invite has expired. Ask the inviter to issue a new one.
errors.bad_signature: This invite's signature didn't verify. Do not accept — it may have been tampered with.
errors.already_redeemed: Someone has already used this invite. Invites are single-use.
errors.self_redeem: You can't redeem an invite you issued yourself. Share the link with someone else.

trust.trustedTooltip: This member has at least two valid vouches.
trust.pendingTooltip: Fewer than two vouches so far. They can still post needs and offers.

trustedBy.title: Vouched for by
trustedBy.intro: These members signed a vouch (or invited this person into the community). Tap a name to see who they are.
trustedBy.empty: No one has vouched for this member yet.

first_exchange.label: First Exchange
first_exchange.description: You completed your first exchange — giving or receiving.

connector_5.label: Connector
connector_5.description: You've helped 5 different people in our community.

regular_4weeks.label: Regular
regular_4weeks.description: You've been active for 4 consecutive weeks — at least one exchange each week.

bridge_builder.label: Bridge Builder
bridge_builder.description: You fulfilled a need in a category no one had filled before.

seed_planter.label: Seed Planter
seed_planter.description: 3 people you invited have each completed an exchange.

listener.label: Listener
listener.description: You've completed 3 exchanges supporting someone emotionally.

weaver.label: Weaver
weaver.description: Your exchanges have connected members across 3 or more areas of our community.

groundbreaker.label: Groundbreaker
groundbreaker.description: You launched a community project that drew at least one contributor.

crew_member.label: Crew Member
crew_member.description: You completed 3 or more confirmed tasks across community projects.

momentum_maker.label: Momentum Maker
momentum_maker.description: A project you organized reached the halfway mark of its target hours.

keystone.label: Keystone
keystone.description: A project you organized was completed.

attention.title: Needs your attention
attention.intro: A few things are waiting on you. Tap to handle them when you have a moment.
attention.tapToConfirm: Tap to confirm and release the credits.
attention.tapToConfirmTask: Tap to confirm and credit the helper.

taskCheckIn.line: Still on “{{task}}” for {{project}}? It's been {{days}} days.
taskCheckIn.hint: Capacity changes — release it if you need to. No record kept.

postClaimed.hint: A helper is on the way. Tap to see the post.

vouchReceived.hint: Each vouch brings you closer to trusted status.

board.title: Community board
board.tagline: Post what you need. Offer what you can.

empty.needs: No needs match these filters yet. If you need help, post something — asking is never gated in a timebank.
empty.offers: No offers match these filters yet. What could you share with your community this week?

dashboard.title: Community dashboard
dashboard.tagline: The unit of progress is us, not me.

categoryBreakdown.title: Where help is flowing
categoryBreakdown.empty: No exchanges yet. Once the first one happens, you'll see the mix of care, labor, and support flowing through the community.

breadth.title: Breadth, not depth
breadth.intro: Members ranked by the number of distinct people they've helped — generosity spread widely, not piled deep.
breadth.empty: No exchanges yet. The first one will start the pattern.
breadth.moreNote: {{count}} more {{noun}} have reached at least one person — every name in the community would belong here if we listed them all.

reciprocity.title: Reciprocity
reciprocity.intro: Connections that flow both ways — A has helped B and B has helped A.
reciprocity.footnote: Not a score. Just a window into the shape of help across the community.

member.notFound: Member not found on this device.
member.vouchIntro: Vouching is a signed statement that this person is part of our community. Two distinct vouches (including invite redemption) move a member from pending-trust to trusted.
member.vouchedJustNow: Vouched. Your signed statement is in the local ledger and (if a community node is configured) on its way there.
member.cannotVouchUntilTrusted: Vouching is enabled once you're trusted yourself. Your inviter's vouch is one; another community member can vouch for you to make it two.
member.alreadyFullyTrusted: This member is already trusted. Another vouch wouldn't change anything.
member.alreadyVouchedByYou: You've already vouched for this member.

balance.label: Your balance
balance.footerNote: Balances can go negative — asking is never gated.
balance.surplus: You've given more than you've received lately. Thank you.
balance.neutral: Your balance is right at your starting seed. That's a fine place to be.
balance.receiving: You've been receiving — that's what seed credits are for. Ask for what you need.

rolesEarned.title: Community roles earned
rolesEarned.empty: You haven't earned any community roles yet. They show up as you participate — not as trophies, but as ways of naming the shapes your contributions take.

history.title: Your exchange history
history.empty: Nothing here yet. When you give or receive help, each exchange shows up with a signed record you can verify.
history.flagShort: Very short exchange — surfaced for community review.
history.flagReciprocal: Repeated reciprocal exchange with the same person — surfaced for community review.

memberSwitcher.title: Switch member (local dev)
memberSwitcher.note: In a real deployment each device holds one identity. This switcher exists so you can walk through the full exchange flow yourself while testing.

data.title: Data & privacy
data.intro: Everything you see is stored locally on this device. No server. No analytics. No account to sign up for.

learn.title: Learn
learn.intro: The welcome flow, common questions, a condensed member guide, and prompts for study groups. Everything here works offline.

communitySettings.title: Community settings
communitySettings.intro: Thresholds the community can tune to fit its own context. Defaults work for most groups.
communitySettings.bootstrapNote: These changes take effect immediately for everyone on this device. Once the in-app proposal system ships, edits here will route through a community vote instead.
communitySettings.proposeLink: Or propose a change for the community to discuss →

dailyLimit.label: Daily helper limit
dailyLimit.hint: The most exchanges any single helper can complete in 24 hours. A hard stop, not a guideline.

shortExchange.label: Short-exchange flag threshold (hours)
shortExchange.hint: Exchanges shorter than this get an advisory flag for community review. Not blocked, not punished — just surfaced.

reciprocal.label: Reciprocal-pattern threshold
reciprocal.hint: When the same two members exchange this many times in 30 days, the latest exchange gets an advisory flag.

taskCheckInDays.label: Task check-in (days)
taskCheckInDays.hint: After this many days, the claimer of a project task gets a private 'still on it?' prompt in their attention list. They can release without anyone seeing.

taskNeedsHelpDays.label: Task 'could use more hands' (days)
taskNeedsHelpDays.hint: After this many days, a claimed task is publicly marked as needing more hands. Framed at the task, not the person. Must be at least the check-in threshold.

taskCheckInGraceDays.label: Task check-in grace (days)
taskCheckInGraceDays.hint: After the claimer acknowledges the private nudge, suppress the public 'could use more hands' chip for this many days. Lets a responsive claimer keep the task out of community signalling. Keep small (1-3) so prolonged silence still surfaces.

deliberationDays.label: Proposal deliberation period (days)
deliberationDays.hint: Minimum days a proposal must stay open before it can auto-pass on consensus. Prevents rush votes.

minAffirms.label: Minimum affirm votes
minAffirms.hint: Distinct affirm votes required for a proposal to auto-pass. Even with deliberation done, a proposal that only one person has affirmed shouldn't pass silently.

language.title: Language
language.intro: Choose how Understoria speaks to you on this device. More languages and translations of the rest of the app are coming as the i18n migration proceeds.

node.title: Community node
node.intro: Optionally mirror your finalized exchanges to a community node so they show up in the community-wide ledger. The node verifies every signature; it never stores your private key.
node.enableLabel: Mirror finalized exchanges to this node

invites.title: Invites you've issued
invites.intro: A new member needs two vouches to become trusted. Your invite counts as the first — someone else will need to vouch for them after they join.
invites.empty: You haven't invited anyone yet. When you do, the invite link will show up here so you can re-share it or revoke it.
invites.shareTitle: Share this link with one person
invites.shareNote: The link is single-use and expires in 14 days. If it leaks before redemption, revoke it below.

security.title: Security
security.summaryProtected: Your secret keys on this device are wrapped with a passphrase-derived key. Unlock is required at every launch.
security.summaryUnprotected: Your secret keys are stored in plaintext on this device. Enable passphrase protection before leaving the device alone in any hostile environment.
security.passphraseHint: At least 8 characters. A four-word pass-phrase from a password manager is ideal. There is no recovery — we cannot get this back for you.
security.disableWarn: This will rewrite your wrapped secret keys as plaintext on this device. Only do this if you're sure the device is safe.
security.successEnable: Passphrase protection enabled. Write your passphrase down somewhere safe — it can't be recovered.
security.successChange: Passphrase changed. Use the new one next time you unlock.
security.successDisable: Passphrase protection disabled. Secret keys are stored in plaintext on this device again.
security.errorMismatchNew: The two new passphrases didn't match.

emergency.title: Emergency
emergency.intro: Two panic options for when a device is at risk. Neither contacts a server; both happen entirely on this device.
emergency.softBullet: Soft purge — strips every identifying text field (names, descriptions, areas, skills) while keeping the signed exchange ledger and your keypair. Useful if a device will briefly be handled by a hostile party.
emergency.hardBullet: Hard purge — wipes every table including private keys, rotates to a fresh node identity. Unrecoverable. The page will reload afterward.
emergency.softConfirmDescription: Identifying text on every member and post will be blanked out. Signed exchange records and your keypair will be preserved. This is not reversible.
emergency.hardConfirmDescription: Every table will be wiped — including your private keys. A fresh node identity will be generated and the app will reload. There is no undo.

help.title: Common questions
help.subtitle: Quick answers for the things members hit while using the app. Looking for the bigger picture? Check the member guide or revisit the welcome from your profile.
help.footer: Didn't find your question? Most of what isn't here is a community conversation, not an app feature — talk to a member you trust.

disputes.title: Disputes
disputes.subtitle: Exchanges someone has flagged for the community to review. Anyone can see this list — there are no admins. The two parties already see the flag on the post itself; this is where it surfaces to everyone else.
disputes.empty: Nothing flagged right now. When someone flags an exchange, it shows here so the community can see and respond.
disputes.footer: Each dispute also shows up in Decisions, where the community can vote on how to respond. Resolution closes the dispute in both places.

entry.title: Community disputes
entry.descriptionNone: No exchanges are flagged right now.
entry.descriptionOne: {{count}} exchange has been flagged for community review.
entry.descriptionOther: {{count}} exchanges have been flagged for community review.

proposals.title: Proposals
proposals.subtitle: Community decisions in progress. Anyone can start one; anyone can record an outcome once the community has agreed. Voting in-app comes later — for now this surface tracks the decisions you made elsewhere.
proposals.footer: Voting and automatic close are still to come. For now the community reaches decisions on whatever channel it uses, and any member can record the outcome here so the historical record reflects reality.
proposals.outcomeReasonPlaceholder: A short note about how the community got here.
proposals.recordOutcomeHint: Community decided already? Record the outcome:
proposals.consensusReached: Consensus reached: {{affirms}} affirm, {{blocks}} blocks. Any member can close this proposal.

closedReason.consensus: Closed by member action after consensus conditions were met.

reversibility.easyTooltip: Another proposal can undo this. Most config tweaks land here.
reversibility.easyHint: Another proposal can undo this. Most config tweaks land here.
reversibility.moderateTooltip: Takes effort to reverse. Worth a pause before passing.
reversibility.moderateHint: Takes effort to reverse. Worth a pause before passing.
reversibility.hardTooltip: Reversal would require rebuilding something. Impact reflection encouraged.
reversibility.hardHint: Reversal would require rebuilding something. Impact reflection is encouraged for hard-tier proposals.

entry.title: Community proposals
entry.descriptionOne: {{count}} proposal open for the community to discuss.
entry.descriptionOther: {{count}} proposals open for the community to discuss.

proposals.errorTitleRequired: Give the proposal a short title.
proposals.errorPositiveInteger: Must be a whole number greater than 0.

eligibility.waitDeliberation: Auto-passes {{when}} if no blocks land before then.
eligibility.waitAffirms: Needs {{need}} affirms to auto-pass on consensus; has {{have}} so far.
eligibility.blocked: Held by {{count}} block — needs to be resolved before this can move forward.

vote.blockReasonPlaceholder: What needs to be resolved before this can move forward?

toast.created: Proposal started. Share it with your community on your usual channel.

new.title: Start a proposal
new.subtitle: Propose a change to your community's node configuration. The proposal stays open until someone records the outcome — that step is manual for now while we get a feel for what proposals actually look like.
new.descriptionPlaceholder: What problem does this solve? Any concerns? How should the community weigh in?
new.configIntro: Edit any of the three thresholds. Unchanged fields stay at their current value.
new.tierIntro: How hard would this be to undo? Defaults to easy because config can be changed back by another proposal — bump to moderate or hard if the cultural impact is bigger than the technical reversal.
new.impactIntro: Hard-to-reverse decisions deserve a pause. All four prompts are required for hard-tier proposals — the point is to slow down and think through each dimension before something that's harder to take back.
new.impactFooter: These notes will be visible to your community alongside the proposal.

impact.yearOneLabel: What changes in the first year?
impact.yearOnePlaceholder: Concrete effects you can already see.
impact.fiveYearPlaceholder: What does this look like once it's settled in?
impact.reversalPathLabel: How would this be undone if it's wrong?
impact.reversalPathPlaceholder: What's the path back? Who'd have to agree?
impact.vulnerableImpactLabel: Who's most affected — especially the most vulnerable members?
impact.vulnerableImpactPlaceholder: Who carries the weight if this goes badly?

timebank.title: This is a timebank
timebank.body1: Help is tracked in hours. One hour of your help equals one hour of anyone else's — whether you're fixing a sink or listening to someone through a hard day.
timebank.body2: There is no pricing. There is no rating. The work that markets undervalue gets the same weight as the work they overvalue.

credit.title: You start with credit
credit.body1: Every new member begins with 5 hours of seed balance. You can ask for help before you've given any.
credit.body2: Asking is not debt. It's how the network comes alive — and how it stays alive when it's your turn to need something.

identity.title: Your identity is a key, not an account
identity.body1: There is no email, no phone number, no password. You are a cryptographic key pair. Your display name is a label you choose; you can change it.
identity.body2: No company owns your identity. No one can lock you out. That also means no one can rescue you if you lose your keys — there's a passphrase option in Profile if that worries you.

community.title: The community is the authority
community.body1: There are no admins. Decisions are made together — on whatever channel your community uses today, and in-app as Understoria grows.
community.body2: If something goes wrong, the response starts with conversation, not bans. Moderation, when it exists, is rotating, transparent, and accountable.

profileSetup.title: A little about you
profileSetup.intro: All optional — these help others find you when they need someone nearby or with a particular skill. You can change any of it later in Profile.
profileSetup.hint: Nothing here is private to the network — only share what you'd share with a neighbor.
